description:
## Problem

`src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr` is **908 lines** and mixes **six distinct concerns** in one module:

1. Concurrency semaphore (acquire/release/health/repair)
2. Per-feed fetch orchestration (single, batch, timeout, fallback)
3. `GCCollector` (incremental + periodic full GC)
4. `FiberTracker` (active/peak/spawn counters)
5. `RefreshHealthMonitor` (cycle start/complete/failure tracking)
6. `State` class + supervisor loop (`start`, `refresh_all`, sleep/heartbeat/health-reporter)
7. Top-level back-compat shims (`refresh_all` free functions, `alias RefreshHealthMonitor`)

A focused audit found issues in three categories — see children for details. This epic tracks the larger refactor that consolidates the work.

## Categories of Issues

### Reliability (correctness)
- `fetch_single_feed_in_background` uses a broad `rescue ex` that **swallows `CancelError`**, breaking the cancel protocol — when the supervisor cancels, the worker treats it as a fetch error and pushes a stale cached feed into the channel.
- `run_initial_refresh` does **not** pass a `cancel_ch`, so the first refresh cannot be cancelled.
- 9 bare `rescue` clauses (no error class) silently turn programming errors into `"unavailable"` / `0` defaults. These hide real bugs.
- `reload_config_if_changed` calls `File.info(config_path).modification_time` without error handling — a missing config file will crash the supervisor.

### DRY (duplication)
- The "use cached data on error, else build an error feed" pattern is **duplicated 4 times** in `fetch_single_feed_with_timeout` (lines 192–219).
- The "interruptible sleep with shutdown check + chunked timeout" pattern is **duplicated 5+ times** across `start`, `start_health_reporter`, `sleep_between_cycles`, `run_initial_refresh`.
- `rescue Channel::ClosedError` boilerplate repeated 5 times around `channel.send`.

### Readability / structure
- 908 LOC file combines 6+ responsibilities — natural seam for splitting.
- Top-level `refresh_all` and `alias RefreshHealthMonitor` shims at end of file are a back-compat hack.
- `MAX_CONSECUTIVE_SKIPS = 3` defined at the **end** of the `State` class body, easy to miss.
- `build_software_releases` is a one-line wrapper around `QuickHeadlines::SoftwareUtil.build_software_releases` with no added value.
- `RefreshHealthMonitor.force_stuck!` is a test helper living on a production class.
- Three modules (`GCCollector`, `FiberTracker`, `RefreshHealthMonitor`) all use `@@class_var` state — hard to test, hard to reset, hard to reason about concurrency.

## Strategy

Fix reliability bugs first (they can ship independently of the refactor), then extract DRY helpers, then split the file.

## Dependency Graph

```mermaid
graph TD
  EPIC[quickhea-XXX Refactor refresh_loop.cr]

  REL1[P1]:::bug -- swallowed CancelError --> EPIC
  REL2[P2] -- initial refresh cancel_ch --> EPIC
  REL3[P1]:::bug -- 9 bare rescues --> EPIC
  REL4[P2] -- File.info error handling --> EPIC

  DRY1[P2] -- fall-back helper --> EPIC
  DRY2[P2] -- interruptible_sleep helper --> EPIC

  READ1[P3] -- file split by concern --> EPIC
  READ2[P3] -- test-only methods out of prod --> EPIC

  DRY1 --> READ1
  DRY2 --> READ1
  REL1 --> READ1
  REL2 --> READ1
  REL3 --> READ1
  REL4 --> READ1
  READ2 --> READ1

  classDef bug fill:#fdd,stroke:#c00
```

## Acceptance

- All child tickets closed
- `just nix-build` passes
- `nix develop . --command crystal spec` passes
- File at most ~300 LOC after `READ-1` lands (down from 908)
- `grep -c "rescue$" src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr` returns 0 (no bare rescues)
- `grep -c "rescue Channel::ClosedError" src/fetcher/*.cr` decreases (DRY win)

## Out of scope

- Changes to `RefreshHealthMonitor`'s public contract (callers in `admin_controller.cr` and `api_base_controller.cr` must keep working)
- Performance tuning of the GC schedule (separate ticket)
- Replacing the concurrency model (Channel-based semaphore) with an Actor
title: Refactor src/fetcher/refresh_loop.cr (908 LOC) for readability and reliability
